metadata.title: States PCS: Mizoram GK for Mizoram PSC Exams

questions.question: Which type of soil is most prevalent in the hilly regions of Mizoram and supports traditional jhum (shifting) cultivation?
questions.explanation: Red loamy soils, formed under tropical monsoon, are predominant in Mizoram and well-suited for jhum cultivation practiced in the region’s hills.

questions.question: Which river serves as the natural boundary between Mizoram and Bangladesh?
questions.explanation: Karnaphuli River originates in Mizoram, forms part of the Mizoram-Bangladesh natural boundary, and flows into Bangladesh.

questions.question: Which is the largest and most agriculturally productive valley in Mizoram?
questions.explanation: Champhai Valley is Mizoram’s largest and most fertile valley, famous for rice and grape cultivation and serving as a major agricultural hub.

questions.question: Which is the largest natural lake in Mizoram, formed due to tectonic activity?
questions.explanation: Palak Lake (Palak Dil) is Mizoram’s largest natural lake, created by tectonic activity and located in Saiha district.

questions.question: Which mountain range extends north to south and is primarily responsible for the rugged terrain of Mizoram?
questions.explanation: Lushai Hills, part of the Patkai range, run north to south and define Mizoram’s distinctive rugged topography.

questions.question: Which country does Mizoram share its eastern international border with?
questions.explanation: Mizoram shares a 510 km border with Myanmar to the east and southeast.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the predominant climate type of Mizoram, influenced by its elevation and the monsoon?
questions.explanation: Mizoram has a humid subtropical climate, marked by heavy monsoon rainfall, moderate temperatures due to elevation, and dense forests.

questions.question: Which pass in Mizoram serves as a key trade and travel route to Myanmar’s Chin State?
questions.explanation: Hnahlan Pass in Champhai district facilitates major border trade and direct connectivity between Mizoram and Chin State, Myanmar under India’s Act East policy.

questions.question: Which national park in Mizoram is situated around the state’s highest peak, Phawngpui?
questions.explanation: Phawngpui National Park is located around Phawngpui peak, the highest point in Mizoram, and is also called Blue Mountain National Park.

questions.question: Which type of forest covers the largest area in Mizoram as a result of its tropical monsoon climate?
questions.explanation: Tropical evergreen forests dominate Mizoram due to high rainfall and humidity, supporting dense biodiversity.
